author: @VeniceInventors
text: To put taxes in context: Not so long ago, kings used to send their soldiers to the farmers to forcibly take bags of grain or other foods, so that they could maintain their lavish lifestyle without ever having to work. The motives and methods haven't changed, but the proportion has increased drastically and the population has gotten so used to getting robbed that most now believe it is necessary.

author: @gerryplayz4532
text: Just basic income tax and vat is 42% up to 25k and 63% on anything above that, then you just have road tax, fuel tax, property tax, inheritance tax, capital gains tax, water tax, air tax etc. it's probably between 70-100 days including the weekends that are "for you".

author: @philip5940
text:
There's context ,but also there is perspective. Eg.... we might be told that federal cats in Australia kill saytwo million small animals each day . Perspective is how many millions of small animals are estimated to exist, how many die of vehicle strikes or fire or starvation due to habitat loss , how many of those small animals are introduced mice and rats , how many are killed by native predators .  I'm  saying big numbers have a mesmerising arresting effect but be wary when your given big numbers without perspective, just like you have to be wary when something is too good to be true and when someone spins a story that evokes a strong emotional response. 
  The sun consumes 600 million tons of hydrogen per second . Scary huh  .  However the sun will continue for another 5 billion years. 
 Yes , cats and foxes are a problem in Australia and I've always found it curious that even scientific bodies like CSIRO resort to bamboozlement and emotional triggers to explain that something needs to be done.

author: @philip5940
text: I remember a report where it was found that Graphene is superconducting when one graphene plane is misaligned by 1.1 degrees from the adjacent plane .
